title: The total after a conservative update changes only by boundary fluxes

source.citation: Randall J. LeVeque, Finite Volume Methods for Hyperbolic Problems, Cambridge University Press, 2002, Ch. 4, pp. 64-65.
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: For a conservative update u_i^+ = u_i - scale * (flux(i+1)-flux(i)), the total over the first n cells equals the old total minus scale times the boundary flux difference flux(n)-flux(0).
assumptions: u and flux are real-valued functions on natural-number grid indices
totalOn sums a state over Finset.range n
informal.expected_conclusion: The updated total differs from the original total only by the scaled boundary flux difference.

declarations.name: totalOn
declarations.statement: def totalOn (u : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) : Real := Finset.sum (Finset.range n) (fun i => u i)

declarations.name: conservativeUpdate
declarations.statement: def conservativeUpdate (scale : Real) (u flux : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) : Real := u i - scale * fluxDifference flux i

declarations.name: totalOn_conservativeUpdate
declarations.statement: theorem totalOn_conservativeUpdate (scale : Real) (u flux : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) : totalOn (fun i => conservativeUpdate scale u flux i) n = totalOn u n - scale * (flux n - flux 0)

tasks.input: Translate the total-balance theorem for a conservative update into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem totalOn_conservativeUpdate (scale : Real) (u flux : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) : totalOn (fun i => conservativeUpdate scale u flux i) n = totalOn u n - scale * (flux n - flux 0)

tasks.input: Given totalOn, conservativeUpdate, and fluxDifference_sum_range, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof using Finset.sum_sub_distrib, Finset.mul_sum, fluxDifference_sum_range, and rewriting.

tasks.input: Find the Mathlib lemmas needed to distribute a finite sum over subtraction and pull out a scalar factor.
